content: "issue a work order to rack tank T3 to tank T4 and assign it to sarah@example.com"

The draft work order is on screen, but it couldn't resolve **sarah@example.com** to a member, so it can't be issued yet. Please confirm the assignee (a matching Cellarhand user) on the card, or let me know the correct email/name.
"put in a work order to top up the barrels that need it"

I can't tell which barrels need topping — that's a judgment call, and "the barrels that need it" isn't something I can resolve to specific vessels. Let me pull the current barrel contents so we can pick the ones to top.Looking at the barrels, here's the headspace situation — the ones with meaningful ullage are the topping candidates:

- **Barrel B3** — 100 L in a 225 L barrel → **~125 L headspace** (2025 Pinot Noir). Clear candidate.
- **Barrel B4** — ~225 L in a 225 L barrel, but it holds 3 MUST lots (still fermenting). Effectively full; you don't top fermenting must.

The rest are at capacity:
- **B1** 225/225, **B2** 225/225, **B5** 228/228, **B6** 500/500 — full, nothing to top.

So the only barrel that clearly needs topping is **B3**. Note B4/B5/B6 hold active MUST (mid-ferment) — those aren't normally topped.

Want me to issue a topping work order for **just B3**? If so, tell me the source vessel to top from (e.g. a keg or tank) and roughly how much, and I'll put it in.

Which "Block 1" — I want to target the right one before deleting. Let me look it up.There are seven "Block 1" blocks across different vineyards — I need you to pick the exact one:

- **Block 1 — Cabernet** in QBO Demo Vineyard zhmfs
- **Block 1 (Sauvignon Blanc)** in Ojai
- **Block 1 (Cabernet Sauvignon)** in Madera
- **Block 1 — Pinot** in Russian River Ranch
- **Block 1 — Cabernet** in QBO Demo Vineyard
- **Block 1 — Cabernet** in QBO Demo Vineyard pt0sk
- **Block 1 — Cabernet** in Oakville Estate

Which vineyard's Block 1 do you want to delete? Note that deleting a block may be blocked if it has Brix readings, picks, or yield estimates attached — I'll relay any blockers before anything is removed.
I want to issue a work order to Mike Juergens to rack wine from tank T3 to tank T4. It should rack all the wine.
The work order is ready to rack all wine (~4,200 L) from **T3 → T4**, assigned to Mike Juergens. There's 1 warning attached — please review and confirm the card to issue it.

I need to know which vineyard's Block 3 — "Block 3" likely exists in several. Let me log it so you get a picker to pin the exact block.It resolved to **Block 3 (Ojai)** at 24.2 °Bx (today). Review and confirm the card — if you meant a different vineyard's Block 3, let me know and I'll re-point it.
log a tasting note on T5 that it smells like rotten eggs right now

I've logged a tasting note on T5's lot flagging the rotten-egg aroma. Review and confirm the card to save it.

Quick heads-up: rotten-egg smell is classic **H₂S (reductive sulfide)**. Want me to pull up sourced guidance on diagnosing/treating it (splash racking, aeration, copper — copper is regulated at the 0.5 mg/L TTB residual cap), or record any lab numbers on the tank?

⚠️ Correction: I have not actually created or filed anything yet — there is no card to confirm and nothing was saved. Tell me to go ahead and I'll do it now.
